calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-02-2024 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-07-2023 Set For Trial On Aug-19-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER to Amended ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-14-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Plaintiff's DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's demurrer to and motion to strike the answer attack some 25 affirmative defenses with various arguments. It is not reasonable to expect the court to hack through this thicket. Counsel (including the senior lawyer for each side) are ordered to meet and confer in good faith to resolve their differences. If complete resolution proves impossible, counsel shall create a unified document that individually addresses each still-at-issue affirmative defense in numerical order as follows: (1) the number, title and text of the defense; (2) all of plaintiff's arguments regarding that particular defense and (3) all of defendants' arguments regarding that defense. Plaintiff may then put her motions back on the court's calendar.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-14-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Plaintiff's MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's demurrer to and motion to strike the answer attack some 25 affirmative defenses with various arguments. It is not reasonable to expect the court to hack through this thicket. Counsel (including the senior lawyer for each side) are ordered to meet and confer in good faith to resolve their differences. If complete resolution proves impossible, counsel shall create a unified document that individually addresses each still-at-issue affirmative defense in numerical order as follows: (1) the number, title and text of the defense; (2) all of plaintiff's arguments regarding that particular defense and (3) all of defendants' arguments regarding that defense. Plaintiff may then put her motions back on the court's calendar.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)
